sound_type: adult bleat -- nasal goat-like 'baaah'. 200-600 Hz. Contact call across alpine cliffs. E=4
ecosystem: Alpine and subalpine. Rocky cliffs, talus slopes, mountain meadows above treeline. 1,500-3,000 m. Cascades.
season: Year-round resident. Rut November-December. Kids born May-June.

coordinates.description: Mountain Goat in Cascades and North Cascades alpine zones. Native population.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST BOVIDAE in the engine. Mountain Goat is NOT a true goat (Capra) -- it's a goat-antelope (Oreamnos), closest to chamois (Rupicapra). Convergent evolution.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Bovidae: Mountain Goat (203, E=4). First entry. Kid at 204.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Oreamnos from Greek 'oros' (mountain) + 'amnos' (lamb). americanus for North America.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Oreamnos -- sole living species. Goat-antelope. Not Capra (true goats).
taxonomy_note.order_note: Artiodactyla. Even-toed ungulates. Third artiodactyl family after Cervidae.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Bovidae: FIRST ENTRY. Largest ungulate family globally. Mountain Goat is PNW representative.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Highest-elevation mammal in the engine.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 200-600 Hz. Low to mid-range. Nasal quality.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Adult bleat: 200-600 Hz. Kid call: 400-1,000 Hz. Billy rut grunt: 100-300 Hz.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Adult bleat: 0.5-1.5 sec. Repeated at intervals for herd contact.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single nasal bleat per vocalization. Repeated as contact call.
song_metrics.note_types: Adult bleat (contact, nasal), kid mew (separation, higher), billy grunt (rut, lower).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Repeated bleats at variable intervals. More frequent when herd spread across terrain.
song_metrics.timbre: Nasal, resonant, goat-like. Cuts through wind. Rock-amplified.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Moderate. Amplified by alpine acoustics. 200-500 m carry on rock.
song_metrics.tempo: Bleats at variable intervals. More frequent during movement across terrain.
song_metrics.context: Contact call. Herd cohesion. Nanny-kid communication. General awareness on alpine cliffs.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Bleats consistent across Cascades populations.
song_metrics.key_property: ADULT BLEAT. Nasal. 200-600 Hz. FIRST BOVIDAE. Not a goat -- goat-antelope. Alpine cliff specialist. E=4. v1.49.344.

pelage.overall: White. Dense double-layered coat: soft undercoat + long guard hairs. Warmest coat in North America. Black horns (both sexes). Black hooves and nose.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: All white. Black horns (both sexes, permanent). Black hooves. Beard-like chin hair. Stocky build.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Winter: thick, shaggy. Summer: shorter, shed undercoat. Always white. Horns year-round.

physical_description.overall: Adult: 45-140 kg (100-300 lbs). 90-120 cm at shoulder. White coat. Black dagger-like horns (15-30 cm, both sexes). Stocky, muscular.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: All-white ungulate on alpine cliffs. Black horns, hooves, nose. Shaggy beard. Cannot be confused with any other PNW mammal in its habitat.
physical_description.field_id: White shaggy animal on high alpine cliffs or talus slopes. Both sexes have horns. Stocky build. If it's white and on a cliff above treeline, it's a Mountain Goat.

nodes.title: FIRST BOVIDAE -- New Family Enters
nodes.description: Bovidae enters the engine. Cattle/goat/sheep/antelope family. Mountain Goat first. Not a true goat -- goat-antelope related to chamois.

nodes.title: Taxonomic Misidentification -- Not a Goat
nodes.description: Oreamnos (goat-antelope) vs. Capra (true goats). Closest to chamois. Convergent evolution. The name is a lie that describes the behavior.

nodes.title: Cliff Fortress Strategy -- Vertical Escape
nodes.description: Can't outrun predators. Goes where they can't follow. Split hooves, rubbery pads, low center of gravity. The cliff is the fortress.

nodes.title: Alpine Acoustics -- Rock Amplification
nodes.description: Rock surfaces reflect sound. Wind is constant. Nasal bleat cuts through wind. E=4 on stone carries like E=5 in forest.

cross_references.relationship: ADULT-KID PAIR! Adult bleat (E=4) and kid mew (E=3). Same species, alpine nursery.
UNGULATE COMPARISON! Elk bugle (E=6, forest valley) vs. goat bleat (E=4, alpine cliff). Different habitats.
PREDATOR-PREY! Bear (predator) vs. Mountain Goat (prey). The cliff is the escape. Bear cannot follow.
MISIDENTIFICATION AND DEFIANCE! Both defy classification. Goat-antelope called a goat. Punk-pop defying genre labels.
